title: Hermanus to house innovative whale centre
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The South African Whale...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - The South African Whale Centre (SAWC) is set to be built in the heart of Hermanus in the Western Cape, the whale capital of South Africa.

The centre will house the Whale Unit of the University of Pretoria’s (UP) Mammal Research Institute (MRI).

The board of trustees of the Old Harbour Museum, in partnership with a dedicated project development team and the UP MRI Whale Unit, recently announced plans to redevelop the Whale House Museum into a cutting-edge SAWC. The Whale House Museum is part of the Old Harbour Museum complex.


> The state-of-the-art facility will be a global beacon for marine conservation and education.


The UP MRI Whale Unit, previously hosted in an off-campus field station of the MRI in Hermanus, will form part of the SAWC when completed.

The SAWC will also feature ground-breaking technology, including a 3D immersive dome experience, interactive exhibits and augmented reality displays that will vividly illustrate whale behaviour and migration.

Visitors will be able to engage in virtual whale encounters and explore the fascinating world of whale songs, fostering a deeper appreciation for these majestic creatures.


> Hub for international whale research


In addition to its educational offerings, the centre will function as a hub for international whale research.

Its partnership with the UP MRI Whale Unit will ensure that the SAWC remains at the forefront of scientific advancements, maximising its educational outreach with the latest research findings.

“UP has been a cornerstone of whale research in Hermanus for many years, and our partnership with the SAWC represents a significant step forward in our shared commitment to marine conservation,” says Dr Els Vermeulen, head of the UP MRI Whale Unit.

“This new centre will not only provide a world-class platform for research but also offer unparalleled educational opportunities, inspiring future generations of marine scientists and conservationists. We are excited to continue our long-standing collaboration and contribute our expertise to the SAWC, ensuring its success as a leading centre for whale research and education.”

“Our mission is to create a world-class facility that not only showcases the beauty of whales but also educates the public on marine conservation. We believe in the power of community, collaboration and legacy to drive awareness and conservation efforts,” adds Grahame Lindop, conceptualisation lead at the SAWC.


> Promoting cetacean conservation


The MRI Whale Unit was established in 1985 as a global, African research, conservation and education facility that researches the ecology, population dynamics and behaviour of the diverse cetaceans (whales, dolphins and porpoises) in the southern African sub-region and surrounding oceans.

Its main objective is to provide knowledge that will promote cetacean conservation.

“For 40 years, the UP MRI Whale Unit has been dedicated to understanding and protecting these incredible creatures,” says Vermeulen.

“The SAWC provides an exciting opportunity to build on this legacy, expanding our research capabilities and reaching a wider audience with our vital conservation message.”
